title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong drove in three runs, aiding the Red Sox in their Saturday win against Cleveland.
Wong's role has diminished due to Mickey Gasper's emergence as the primary catcher under interim manager Chad Tracy.
Wong currently maintains a .245 batting average, which is higher than teammate Carlos Narvaez's .210.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings, striking out seven in Red Sox's 9-1 victory over the Cleveland Guardians.
Throughout May, Gray maintained a 2.00 ERA across five starts, improving his season ERA to 3.06.
Gray's performance contributed to the Red Sox being 7-3 in his starts, earning him six wins this year.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's first annual charity softball game occurs Sunday at Polar Park in Worcester.
Patriots coaches Mike Vrabel and Josh McDaniels will manage Team Patriots and Team Sox respectively.
Funds raised will support the MayeDay foundation, assisting children and families in hardship.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots expected to acquire Eagles' A.J. Brown, pending framework completion and salary cap considerations post-June 1.
Sports Illustrated's Albert Breer reports a trade framework exists but NFL Network's Ian Rapoport says deal is not close.
Philadelphia seeks a first-round pick for Brown; Patriots reportedly hesitant to trade a 2027 first-rounder.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Residents across Greater Boston reported hearing a loud boom at approximately 2:10 PM on Saturday.
Meteorologists identified a bolide meteor as the likely source of the sonic boom heard in the region.
No earthquake was detected, and local officials confirmed there were no related emergency incidents reported.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox relief pitcher Tyler Samaniego allowed all four runs in the first inning in a 4-3 loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts, maintaining a 0.71 ERA in his new role.
The Red Sox are 10 games under .500 and have lost six of their last seven games, now last in AL East.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Boston Celtics fans and the TD Garden's passionate environment during a recent livestream.
Despite not being favorites to land Antetokounmpo, Boston Celtics are linked to him alongside the Miami Heat.
Speculation surrounds a potential Celtics trade involving Jaylen Brown to acquire Antetokounmpo amid ongoing rumors.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police cleared the Harvard Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge over crowd concerns as students attempted to swim in the Charles River.
Cambridge, Harvard University, and Massachusetts State Police responded to the event, ending the gathering shortly after 5 a.m.
The annual event follows commencement speeches, including one by Harvard alumnus Conan O’Brien for the Class of 2026.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair" is now streaming on Peacock, featuring Uma Thurman's acclaimed performance.
"The Truman Show," starring Jim Carrey, is available on Paramount+ and YouTube, highlighting the reality TV genre.
"The Four Seasons" Season 2, starring Tina Fey and Will Forte, is streaming on Netflix, continuing the comedy-drama series.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: ESPN's Kiley McDaniel predicts the Red Sox will draft third baseman Bo Lowrance, 18, in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Lowrance, out of South Carolina, is compared to Freddie Freeman and is projected to be a first-round pick.
Lowrance, committed to the University of Virginia, stands 6'5" and might switch from third base to first base.
